summary: First-run end-to-end testing of 'Wanted for Fries: Angel the Stunt Driver'. All 10 backend pytest tests pass (GET /api/, POST /api/radio for early/mid/late/invalid/empty, POST /api/scores, GET /api/scores/top with no ObjectId leakage). Frontend Playwright E2E (mobile viewport 420x820) verified all critical flows: title screen text + Start Chase, intro story + Floor It, gameplay HUD (distance/fries/hits), player car renders, keyboard ArrowLeft/Right movement, on-screen left/right buttons movement, distance counter increase, AI-generated radio chatter update (saw a Claude-generated line: 'Unit-7 to Dispatch suspect vehicle fleeing at high speed possible armed french fry situation over'), game-over ticket modal with correct distance/fries fields, Pay Fine & Retry restart resets state, and Title button returns to title screen.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: logger is defined AFTER it is referenced inside get_radio_line's except block (line 123 uses logger, defined at line 157). Works only because the reference resolves at call-time (after module init). Recommend moving logger setup to the top of the file before usage for clarity and to avoid future foot-guns.
server.py /api/scores: no input validation on negative distance/fries values; consider Field(ge=0) on the Pydantic model. Not a blocker.

server.py /api/radio: LLM calls have an 8s frontend timeout but no explicit server-side timeout/cap on LlmChat.send_message — could pile up under load. Acceptable for an arcade game; consider documenting.
Game.jsx is 533 lines and approaching the 700-line threshold; if more features are added (leaderboard UI, settings), split into Title/Intro/GameOver/HUD/GameLoop modules.
Game.jsx uses entitiesRef directly in render without a state copy; functional, but be aware that React's reconciliation here relies on forceRender which is fine for an arcade loop.
seed_data_creation: Inserted two TEST_ prefixed score records via /api/scores during test_save_score_returns_record and test_save_another_score (player='TEST_Angel'/'TEST_Angel2'). No cleanup endpoint exists. Safe to ignore — they only affect the public /scores/top leaderboard ordering if distance >= other entries.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Game uses mobile-portrait shell (max-width 448px); use viewport ~420x820. The game loop spawns obstacles at intervals of 380-900ms based on speed and game-over occurs after 3 hits — typically ~15-30s into gameplay if not dodging perfectly. Radio updates fire ~1.2s after game start (initial fetch) and every ~8.5s after. AI radio works with EMERGENT_LLM_KEY; the 'source' field in /api/radio responses is 'ai' when LLM succeeds, 'fallback' otherwise. Backend leaks no Mongo _id (uses {_id:0} projection). Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_wanted_for_fries.py covers all endpoints.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. Initial Playwright run hit a race where the 13s radio-update wait outlasted gameplay (3 hits → gameover within ~13s), causing the radio-box selector to disappear. Resolved in second run by actively dodging via lane buttons while sampling the radio in 600ms intervals.
